description_long: This is a traditional Japanese-style wooden lantern, often found in urban parks, temple grounds, or along walkways. The lantern features a pitched roof with layered wooden shingles, supported by a sturdy wooden frame that flares outward at the base for stability. The central section is enclosed with translucent panels, likely made of rice paper or frosted glass, and displays vertical calligraphic script on the front and a stylized animal illustration on the back. The wood is dark and weathered, giving the lantern an aged, authentic appearance. The structure is elevated above the ground by a wide, sloped wooden base, which helps protect the lantern from moisture and wear. Decorative elements are minimal, focusing on the elegance of the wood grain and the artistry of the inscriptions.
description: A traditional Japanese wooden lantern with a pitched roof, translucent calligraphy panels, and a wide, sloped base. The lantern features dark wood and minimal decorative elements, emphasizing its classic design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the calligraphic script on the translucent panel and the wide, sloped wooden base.
description_view_1: Side/rear view highlighting the pitched roof and a stylized animal illustration on the back panel.
description_view_2: Another front view, similar to view 0, with clear visibility of the calligraphic script and wooden structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side/rear view, again showing the animal illustration and the layered roof design.
